records.summary: A cath-lab monitor displays four fluoroscopic frames from a coronary angiogram procedure performed on April 4, 2026. The images show the coronary arteries from multiple oblique views, including one frame highlighting a 99% stenosis and another showing a stent deployed.
key_observations: Four angiographic frames from a coronary procedure, dated April 4, 2026.
Annotations indicate 'Stenosis 99%' and 'Stent' in separate frames.
Multiple projection angles are shown: LAO 40°/CRAN 2°, LAO 41°/CRAN 1°, RAO 40°/CAUD 35°, and RAO 2°/CRAN 45°.

records.summary: This is a typed PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) report for a 56-year-old female patient. The procedure was successfully performed via transradial access on the right coronary artery (RCA) to treat a 99% stenosis. A stent and balloon were deployed, achieving TIMI-III flow. The report includes patient details, procedure specifics, and a recommendation for follow-up.
key_observations: Patient is a 56-year-old female.
Procedure: Successful PTCA on the RCA using a 1-DES stent.
Lesion: 99% stenosis in the distal RCA, 18mm in length.
Stent deployed: Insignia Neo 3.50x20mm at 12 ATM.
Balloon used: Apollo 3.50x12mm at 18 ATM.
Flow achieved: TIMI-III.
Diagnosis: IWM I, CAD-SVD, Primary PCI to RCA (1-DES).
Recommendation: Regular medicines and follow-up.

records.stage_evidence: Report includes diagnosis and recommendation for PCI, indicating procedure completion.

records.summary: This is a typed coronary angiography report for a 56-year-old female patient. The report details the clinical profile, catheter used, and findings of the coronary anatomy, including a 99% lesion in the distal RCA. The diagnosis is IWMI and CAD-SVD, with a recommendation for primary PCI to the RCA.
key_observations: Patient is a 56-year-old female.
Coronary anatomy findings: LMCA, LAD, LCX, OM, PDA & PLV are normal except for a 99% lesion in the distal RCA (18mm length).
Diagnosis: IWMI (Inferior Wall Myocardial Infarction) and CAD-SVD (Coronary Artery Disease - Single Vessel Disease).
Recommendation: Primary PCI to RCA (1-DES).

records.summary: A four-panel coronary angiogram display from a cath-lab monitor showing sequential images of the coronary arteries during a procedure. Images show a 99% stenosis and a deployed stent in different projections.
key_observations: Four fluoroscopic images from a coronary angiogram procedure.
Image 30/59 (Series 3) shows a 99% stenosis in the left coronary system.
Image 22/40 (Series 26) shows a stent deployed in the same vessel.
Multiple projections: LAO 40°/CRAN 2°, LAO 41°/CRAN 1°, RAO 40°/CAUD 35°, RAO 2°/CRAN 45°.
Study date: 04-Apr-2026.

limitations: Image is a photograph of a monitor screen, not a direct medical image.
